title: Hybrid 3 composite-tool infrastructure for pi-agent-dispatch — per-KIND triples (library + Pi tool + CLI script) + 5-layer forbidden-wholesale enforcement + author-tool-grant Pi tool
description: Build the bounded-composite-vocabulary layer of FEAT-005 capability composition (operation-granular from empty). pi-agent-dispatch's extension declares a small set of canonical composite KINDS in framework code (initial 4: read-files, git-log, grep-paths, command-allowlist); config.tool_operations[] declares INSTANCES of those kinds (each instance = {canonical_id, kind, instance_params: {allowed_roots / since / paths / etc.}}); extension-load reads config + dynamically registers a Pi tool per instance (name=canonical_id, params closure-bound from instance_params). Per-KIND dual-surface (DEC-0019/0020): each KIND lands as a TRIPLE — library function in packages/pi-agent-dispatch/src/composites/{kind}.ts + the Pi tool registration that wraps it + a CLI script at scripts/orchestrator/composite-{kind}.ts taking the same instance_params as CLI flags. Adding a new KIND = source change + release; adding an INSTANCE of an existing KIND = config write under writer.kind=human (via new author-tool-grant Pi tool). 5-layer forbidden-wholesale enforcement prevents the design from degrading silently: L1 framework TS const FORBIDDEN_WHOLESALE_OPERATIONS = ['bash','write','edit','shell','execute']; L2 engine test invariant in operation-vocab.test.ts (TOOL_OPERATION_DEFAULTS contains zero forbidden tokens); L3 runtime guard at extension load reading L1 ∪ L5 union (refuses to load if any registered op is in the union); L4 JSDoc citation at operation-vocab.ts naming the discipline + cross-referencing feedback_no_parallel_ungated_paths; L5 substrate config.tool_operations_forbidden[] for project-specific additions, gated by author-tool-grant writer.kind=human, validated via AmendRegistry descriptor {kind: 'string-array'}. The author-tool-grant Pi tool mirrors TASK-089 author-agent-spec pattern: wraps amendConfigEntry mutations on config.tool_operations[] + config.tool_operations_forbidden[]; refuses writer.kind != 'human' with DEC-0047 message; refuses ops in FORBIDDEN_WHOLESALE_OPERATIONS. Zero canon evolution required.
motivation: Address FGAP-116 (operation-granular capability vocabulary lacks composite-tool authoring + per-KIND dual-surface CLI scripts + forbidden-wholesale enforcement). FEAT-005 today implements name-only granularity via composeToolGrant + operation-vocab.ts (55 Pi tool names). JI-023 user verbatim names finer granularity ('A bash command. Not bash.' + 'There can be a registry of them') and FEAT-006 north-star requires bounded-composite scoping (constrained orchestrator + privileged agent + bounded grant). Composite-tool infrastructure is the concrete realization. Refined v2 design from analysis/2026-05-28-hybrid-3-refined-composite-tools-design.md closes both design-completeness items surfaced in the v1 canon-eval (analysis/2026-05-28-hybrid-3-composite-tools-canon-eval.md commit 3b4b32d).
acceptance_criteria: Per-KIND triple lands together for each of 4 initial KINDS (read-files / git-log / grep-paths / command-allowlist): library function in packages/pi-agent-dispatch/src/composites/{kind}.ts + Pi tool registration at extension-load reading config.tool_operations[] entries of that kind + CLI script at scripts/orchestrator/composite-{kind}.ts taking the same instance_params as CLI flags

config.schema.json extended with tool_operations_forbidden array property (items: string); samples/conception.json mirrors; AmendRegistry union + REGISTRY_DESCRIPTORS in packages/pi-context/src/context.ts include tool_operations_forbidden as kind:string-array

author-tool-grant Pi tool registered in pi-agent-dispatch: wraps amendConfigEntry mutations on tool_operations[] + tool_operations_forbidden[]; refuses writer.kind != 'human' per DEC-0047; refuses operations whose canonical_id is in FORBIDDEN_WHOLESALE_OPERATIONS union (L1 ∪ L5)
L1 forbidden-wholesale framework TS const FORBIDDEN_WHOLESALE_OPERATIONS = ['bash', 'write', 'edit', 'shell', 'execute'] in operation-vocab.ts with JSDoc citation

L2 engine test invariant in operation-vocab.test.ts asserts TOOL_OPERATION_DEFAULTS contains zero entries whose canonical_id is in FORBIDDEN_WHOLESALE_OPERATIONS; husky pre-commit fails on any addition

L3 runtime guard at pi-agent-dispatch/src/index.ts extension-load reads L1 ∪ L5 union; throws extension-load error if any registered op (from TOOL_OPERATION_DEFAULTS or from config.tool_operations[] instances) is in the union

composite-loader.ts: at extension-load reads config.tool_operations[]; for each entry of kind matching a registered KIND, registers a Pi tool wrapping that kind's library function with instance_params closure-bound. Tool name = entry's canonical_id; refuses to register if canonical_id is in L1 ∪ L5 union

Engine unit tests cover each KIND library function (success + error paths + bounds enforcement per instance_params); each Pi tool registration (correct name + param schema + dispatch to library); composite-loader (loads entries + skips kind-mismatch + refuses forbidden-wholesale); author-tool-grant (refuses non-human writer + refuses forbidden-wholesale + happy path)
Each CLI script under scripts/orchestrator/composite-{kind}.ts invokes the same library function as its Pi tool sibling; CLI tested via runtime-demo (.ts test script using tsx)

Zero new pi-workflows/pi-behavior-monitors deps (FEAT-010 lives entirely in pi-agent-dispatch + pi-context schema extension); per DEC-0048 bundled-artifact tests are not regression gates; engine unit tests + per-KIND library tests + composite-loader tests + author-tool-grant tests are gates

VALIDATION = real checks per DEC-0018: build/check/test exit codes (engine + new package + new tests all green); runtime demo per KIND (tsx-eval invokes each CLI script + verifies bounds-enforcement); adversarial probe (fresh-context agent verifies zero forbidden-wholesale tokens registered + L3 runtime guard catches violation attempts + author-tool-grant refuses non-human writers + no parallel ungated paths)
